summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting on July 25, 2023 at 6 p.m. Key topics included updates on Fire Station 18 construction, the damaged medic unit from an accident, acceptance of a $26,400 tech grant from the State of Ohio for drone pilot training, a $100 donation, donation of out-of-service fire gear overseas, and a resident's noise complaint. The board also adjourned into executive session regarding personnel matters.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (July 11–24, 2023) – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 6:07 PM regarding personnel matters – passed
Motion to approve less-lethal/shotgun bean bag instructor training – passed
Motion to accept $100 donation to Goshen Township – passed
Motion to accept tech grant of $26,400 from the State of Ohio for drone pilot certifications – passed
Motion to donate out-of-service fire gear to American Regulators (501c3) for overseas distribution – passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter Ian Smith – passed
Motion to adjourn the meeting at 7:26 PM – passed
action_items: Someone to contact Victoria York regarding her ongoing noise complaint at Lakeshore Estate Mobile Home Park
Fire department to work with insurance company on sourcing a replacement chassis for the damaged medic unit
Awaiting building department approval for Fire Station 18 (Colerain fire documents forwarded)
Trustees to explore making retention and benefits packages more robust to retain firefighters/personnel
Trustee Allen plans to attend Clermont County Fair and meet Lt. Governor John Husted regarding township concerns
key_amounts: $26,400 – tech grant from State of Ohio for drone pilot training/certifications for 16 individuals
notable_topics: Fire Station 18 construction update – awaiting building department approval
Damaged medic unit from accident – chassis totaled, estimated 2 years for full replacement
National Night Out event August 1 at Mark Cook Elementary
Lt. Governor John Husted visit to Clermont County Fair July 27
$26,400 state tech grant for drone pilot certifications
Donation of out-of-service fire gear to overseas program
Resignation of part-time firefighter Ian Smith
Less-lethal shotgun/bean bag instructor training approval
Noise complaint from Victoria York at Lakeshore Estate Mobile Home Park
Discussion on firefighter retention and benefits
Undocumented driver accident involving life squad on Route 28
attendees: Trustee Hodges (Dan – absent, at Clermont County Fair duties)
Labor attorney (unnamed, present for executive session)
notes: Trustee Dan Hodges was absent due to Clermont County Fair Board duties and demolition derby night; the meeting was not rescheduled due to scheduling conflicts. The transcript contains significant audio/transcription artifacts and repetitions, particularly in the second half, making some agenda items and motions difficult to parse clearly. A labor attorney was present for the executive session. The fire chief noted concerns about Chinese-made drones and related regulations. Discussion about the medic unit accident included pointed commentary about the at-fault driver being undocumented without a license or insurance.
title: BOT 07.25.23
